channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Heavy security after deadly violence in Kenya as thousands turn out to mourn former PM Odinga
item.description: Heavy security has been deployed to Kenya’s western city of Kisumu ahead of the final public viewing of the body of former Prime Minister Raila Odinga, after five people died in the last two public funeral events.
item.dc:creator: Evelyne Musambi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UK sees lessons from Northern Ireland that could aid the fragile Gaza ceasefire process
item.description: LONDON (AP) — As Israelis and Palestinians wait anxiously to see what comes after a ceasefire in the two-year war in Gaza, the experience of the Northern Ireland peace process in the 1990s may offer lessons in the thorny process of moving from seemingly intractable conflict to enduring peace.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Deadly floods in Mexico another sign of need for improved severe weather warnings
item.description: Torrential rains in east-central Mexico last week have left at least 72 dead and raised concerns about the government's ability to alert people to severe weather.
item.dc:creator: María Verza, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Prince Andrew gives up his Duke of York title as Epstein allegations refuse to fade
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Prince Andrew said Friday he is giving up his royal title of the Duke of York and other honors after his friendship with sex offender Jeffrey Epstein returned to the headlines.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas defends executions of Palestinians after Trump issues stern warning to terror group
item.description: After Trump’s threat, Hamas official Mohammed Nazzal defends the recent executions of Palestinians in Gaza as the group seeks to settle scores with opponents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy pitches Trump on Ukraine drone-for-Tomahawk missile exchange as president weighs escalation concerns
item.description: President Donald Trump shows interest in a potential drone deal with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy but calls Tomahawk missiles a significant escalation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Daily struggles persist in Gaza even as ceasefire offers some respite
item.description: The Israel-Hamas war ceasefire has provided some respite for Palestinians in Gaza after two years of killings and destruction.
item.dc:creator: Toqa Ezzidin, Wafaa Shurafa And Mariam Fam, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: French police arrest 4 in alleged plot targeting exiled Russian activist and Putin critic
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Police in France detained four people suspected in a plot targeting exiled Russian rights activist Vladimir Osechkin, who exposes abuses in Russian prisons, France's national anti-terror prosecution office said.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER and EMMA BURROWS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Senators look to block Trump from engaging in ‘hostilities’ in Venezuela
item.description: Senators Tim Kaine and Rand Paul lead bipartisan effort to prevent President Donald Trump from launching military strikes in Venezuela without Congress approval.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watch live: Trump meets with Ukraine's Zelensky at White House
item.description: President Trump will host Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky at the White House on Friday for the third time since returning to office in January. The first visit was a train wreck; the second, which involved European leaders, went more smoothly.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, The Hill

item.title: King to pray with pope in first since 1534
item.description: VATICAN CITY/LONDON, Oct 17 (Reuters) - Britain's King Charles and Queen Camilla will meet Pope Leo at the Vatican next week in a visit seen as a sign of closening ties between the Catholic Church and the Anglican Communion, five centuries after their turbulent separation.
item.dc:creator: Joshua McElwee and Muvija M, NewsNation

item.title: Thatcher centennial celebration examines how Reagan partnership guides modern US-UK relations
item.description: Experts examine how the Cold War alliance between President Ronald Reagan and Prime Minister Margaret Thatcher influences today's U.S.-U.K. relationship amid Russian President Vladimir Putin's threats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Europe's main center-left political group expels Slovak leader's party for breaching its values
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Europe’s main center-left political group on Friday kicked out the party of Slovakian Prime Minister Robert Fico, who is accused of cozying up to Russian President Vladimir Putin and undermining the rule of law in his own country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelensky meets with Energy secretary, US companies ahead of visit with Trump
item.description: Ahead of his upcoming meeting with President Trump on Friday, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said he met with Department of Energy Secretary Chris Wright and various American energy and defense companies on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: After years of socialist rule, Bolivia’s presidential runoff tests how far and fast it veers right
item.description: After nearly two decades of one-party rule and a worsening currency and fuel crisis, Bolivia is shifting to the right.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Debre And Carlos Valdez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US military drone strike on drug 'submersible' in Caribbean leaves survivors, official confirms
item.description: U.S. military drone strike destroyed a drug-smuggling submersible in the Caribbean Thursday, leaving 2 to 3 survivors in the water, a U.S. official tells Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Child watching surgery allegedly drilled into patient’s skull
item.description: The unnamed physician denies the accusations but admits to bringing her daughter into an operating room at Graz Regional Hospital.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: UN condemns military takeover in Madagascar as coup leader to be quickly sworn in as president
item.description: ANTANANARIVO, Madagascar (AP) — The United Nations chief on Thursday condemned the military takeover in Madagascar as an army colonel who led the coup was set to be quickly sworn in as president just three days after announcing soldiers had taken charge. U.N.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia targets Ukraine energy facilities ahead of Trump meeting with Zelensky
item.description: Russia hit Ukraine’s energy facilities with hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles Thursday, a day ahead of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky’s meeting with President Trump at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: French pair imprisoned in Iran over alleged spying have 'reached the limit,' families say
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The families of two French nationals detained in Iran for more than three years said their loved ones have reached “the limit of what they can endure” following a report that an Iranian court sentenced them to decades in prison on spying charges.
item.dc:creator: CATHERINE GASCHKA and THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The EU unveils a plan to be ready within 5 years to fend off any Russian attack
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The European Union on Thursday laid out a plan aimed at ensuring that Europe can defend itself against outside attack by the end of the decade as concern mounts that Russia is already probing the bloc’s defenses.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Remains of last female Hamas hostage and IDF soldier handed over to Israel
item.description: Israel received two more deceased hostages' bodies, including that of Inbar Hayman, who was considered to be the last female hostage in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel receives remains of 2 more hostages as military says another body was not that of a hostage
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel received the remains of two more hostages Wednesday, hours after the Israeli military said that one of the bodies previously turned over was not that of a hostage. The confusion added to tensions over the fragile truce that has paused the two-year war.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says Modi told him India would stop buying Russian oil
item.description: President Trump said Wednesday that Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi told him that India would stop buying Russian oil, a potentially significant move to further pressure Moscow over its war in Ukraine. "I was not happy that India was buying oil.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump confirms CIA authorization for operations in Venezuela
item.description: It is unclear whether any operations are planned or if the authorization serves as a contingency measure.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Turkey deports peaceful Christians under guise of ‘national security’ claims watchdog
item.description: Human rights advocate warns Turkey is systematically targeting Christians for deportation, with President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan's policies facing international scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukrainian officials meet with US weapons manufacturers before Trump-Zelenskyy talks
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Ukrainian government delegation has met with prominent American weapons manufacturers during a U.S. visit, a senior Kyiv official said Wednesday, before President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s meeting with U.S. counterpart Donald Trump at the White House later this week.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Afghanistan, Pakistan announce ceasefire after civilians killed
item.description: ISLAMABAD/KABUL, Oct 15 (Reuters) - Pakistan and Afghanistan agreed to a "temporary ceasefire" on Wednesday, Islamabad said, after an airstrike and ground fighting sent tensions between the South Asian neighbors soaring, killing more than a dozen civilians.
item.dc:creator: By Saleem Ahmed, Mushtaq Ali and Mohammad Yunus Yawar, NewsNation

item.title: Remains released by Hamas not a hostage, Israel's military says
item.description: The original framework of a U.S.-brokered ceasefire required Hamas to return all 28 deceased hostages Monday.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s swipe at ‘weak’ leaders highlights the shifting global influence of Western nations
item.description: Amid Trump’s visit to Egypt and Israel to celebrate the release of hostages held by Hamas and to usher in a new era of Mideast peace, some say Western influence is waning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amy Bradley’s family unaware how new leads in case developed: Brother
item.description: Brad Bradley described as "purely specualtive" one of the new leads in the investigation that his sister Amy Bradley potentially has a child.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: Russia may change law, call up 2 million reservists to fight in Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW, Oct 14 (Reuters) - Russia will be able to deploy around two million military reservists to fight in Ukraine if needed under amendments to the law likely to be backed by parliament, giving it greater flexibility to ramp up the size of its force there.
item.dc:creator: By Andrew Osborn, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's power worldwide has 'increased 1,000%': Bill O'Reilly
item.description: Sunday marked 737 days since the hostages were taken, and all 20 hostages remaining alive were released Monday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Schumer, Clinton give Trump credit for Gaza ceasefire, hostage release
item.description: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and former President Clinton gave President Trump credit for the ceasefire in Gaza and the return of Israeli hostages by Hamas. “Today is a wonderful day.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: After hostages and prisoners freed, complex issues remain for Israel-Hamas
item.description: Issues such as whether Hamas will disarm and who will govern Gaza — and the question of Palestinian statehood — remain unresolved, highlighting the fragility of an agreement that for now only pauses the deadliest conflict in the history of Israel and the Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy to visit Washington this week seeking long-range weapons and a Trump meeting
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Monday he will travel to the United States this week for talks on the potential U.S. provision of long-range weapons, a day after U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: From a hilltop village and across Israel: Tears of joy as the living hostages are returned
item.description: LAVON, Israel (AP) — It was a day of joy and tears across Israel. Families, loved ones and friends watched as Hamas released the last living hostages held captive for over two years in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, WGN 9
